response.justification: Candidate has relevant geospatial domain experience (GISCollective, Green Map) and prior low-latency systems work at Sociomantic handling high-throughput services, but the core technical requirement — modern C++ or Rust — is not demonstrated. C/C++ work is roughly a decade old (National Instruments, 2014-2015) and recent roles are web-focused (TypeScript/Node.js). DLang systems experience shows aptitude for low-level, performance-sensitive code, which could transfer, but a C++-first routing tiling role likely needs stronger, more current evidence. Worth a screening conversation only if the team accepts ramp-up time on C++/Rust.
strengths: Direct geospatial/GIS domain experience via GISCollective and Green Map
Prior low-latency, high-throughput backend work at Sociomantic with async I/O and fibers
Long senior-level track record with strong testing/TDD discipline
concerns: No recent modern C++ experience; last C/C++ role was ~2015
Current career gap and recent stack is web/TypeScript, far from systems programming
